name: Wild Boar Trail
name_original: Rota do Javali (PR 2) — Serra da Estrela
description: Hike the challenging 7.2-mile Boar Route (PR 2) in Serra da Estrela Natural Park, featuring forests, boulders, and Poço do Inferno waterfall
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Manteigas (São Pedro) are set within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, Portugal's highest mountain range. The region is characterized by its glacial past, featuring the U-shaped Zêzere Glacial Valley, granite formations, and numerous glacial lagoons. Waterfalls, such as the prominent Poço do Inferno, are a key natural attraction, fed by rivers like the Zêzere. The landscape also includes varied vegetation, from beech groves to dense pine forests. Best waterfall hiking trails around Manteigas (São Pedro) The most popular waterfall hiking route is Wild Boar Trail, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 4 minutes to complete, navigating through forests and boulder fields towards the Poço do Inferno waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Poço do Inferno Route (PR 1) — Serra da Estrela, a difficult 1.7 miles (2.8 km) path. This route leads directly to the impressive 10-meter Poço do Inferno cascade. Local hikers also love the View of Manteigas – View of the Zêzere Valley loop from Manteigas, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through the Zêzere Glacial Valley, often completed in about 4 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Manteigas (São Pedro) is defined by dramatic glacial valleys, towering granite formations, and impressive waterfalls. The network offers options for both easy walks and more challenging, difficult routes. The routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Manteigas (São Pedro)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are there in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The Manteigas (São Pedro) region offers a selection of 5 dedicated waterfall hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes are set within the dramatic landscapes of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go waterfall hiking in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The ideal time for waterfall hiking in Manteigas (São Pedro) is typically between May and September. During these months, the weather is warmer and there's less rainfall, making for more pleasant trail conditions. However, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2132214444/poco-do-inferno-route-pr-1-serra-da-estrela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço do Inferno waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is also known to freeze solid in harsh winters, offering a unique spectacle for those prepared for colder conditions.

Q: Are there any easy waterfall hikes suitable for beginners in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Yes, there are easy options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32161854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Zêzere Valley – Poço do Inferno Waterfall loop from Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9 km (1.8 miles) route that takes approximately 55 minutes to complete, offering a gentle way to experience the area's natural beauty.

Q: Are the waterfall trails in Manteigas (São Pedro) suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the trails are suitable for families, especially the easier routes. The region's diverse landscape, including the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço do Inferno waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, offers engaging scenery for all ages. Consider routes with shorter durations and less elevation gain for younger children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the waterfall hiking trails in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially near steep drops or in areas with livestock. Always check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see along the waterfall trails?
A: Beyond the impressive waterfalls like Poço do Inferno, you'll encounter dramatic glacial landscapes, including the U-shaped Zêzere Glacial Valley, towering granite formations, and picturesque beech groves. The region is also dotted with glacial lagoons such as Lagoa Comprida and Lagoa Escura, adding to the scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall walks in the Manteigas (São Pedro) area?
A: Yes, there are circular options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32161854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Zêzere Valley – Poço do Inferno Waterfall loop from Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt; is an easy circular route that allows you to experience the waterfall and surrounding valley without retracing your steps.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the waterfall trails in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Parking is generally available at common starting points for trails, such as near the Poço do Inferno waterfall or in Manteigas itself. It's recommended to check specific route details for designated parking areas to ensure convenient access to your chosen trail.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the waterfall trails in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The waterfall trails in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 375 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic glacial valleys, the impressive waterfalls, and the diverse terrain that ranges from easy walks to more challenging routes.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the waterfalls?
A: During warmer months, the Rio Zêzere, which feeds many of the waterfalls, offers natural pools where visitors can enjoy wild swimming. Always exercise caution and check local conditions before entering the water.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions near the waterfall trails?
A: Beyond the waterfalls, you can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, visit the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt;, or even venture to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1258525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre (Serra da Estrela Summit)&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The region is rich in natural and historical sites.

Q: How challenging are the waterfall hikes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The trails vary in difficulty. While there are easy options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32161854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Zêzere Valley – Poço do Inferno Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, many routes are considered more challenging. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2132196645/wild-boar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wild Boar Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.6 km (7.2 miles) route with significant elevation gain, navigating through forests and boulder fields.
tips: When granite and slate meet under high pressure and high temperature, hornfels is formed. The result is these pointed rock formations.
The scenery is great. Weather change always possible.
Manteigas is a small village located in the heart of the Serra da Estrela. Many hiking tours start from here.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Boar Route (PR 2) — Serra da Estrela?
faqs.answer: The Boar Route (PR 2) is generally rated as moderate, though komoot classifies it as difficult. It features many steep inclines and some trickier sections that require careful negotiation. Hikers should possess a good level of fitness to comfortably complete the trail.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and end?
faqs.answer: The Boar Route (PR 2) is a circular trail that begins and ends in the town of Manteigas, located within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park.

faqs.question: Is there parking available at the trailhead in Manteigas?
faqs.answer: Yes, Manteigas, being the starting and ending point for this circular route, offers parking options for hikers. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient spots.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail starts with a dirt track featuring steep inclines. You'll then enter a dense forest with oak, chestnut, and pine trees, followed by areas characterized by gigantic boulders and rocks. The landscape also includes humanized elements like old forest houses and a nursery.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Boar Route (PR 2)?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the impressive Poço do Inferno (Hell's Mouth) waterfall, especially striking in winter. You'll also encounter the View of Hornfels Peaks, Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela, the View of the Leandres Valley, and a View of Manteigas, offering picturesque panoramic vistas.

faqs.question: Is the Boar Route (PR 2) — Serra da Estrela dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Serra da Estrela Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be hiked year-round, but each season offers a different experience. Winter brings a more impressive Poço do Inferno waterfall, but can also mean colder, potentially snowy conditions. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is generally warm and dry. Always check weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Serra da Estrela Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required to hike trails like the Boar Route (PR 2) within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations regarding waste, fire, and wildlife.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Boar Route (PR 2) is part of the extensive network of trails in Serra da Estrela. It fully encompasses the Rota do Javali and also passes through sections of the Grande Rota das Aldeias Históricas - Variante and the Grande Rota do Zêzere.

faqs.question: What is the recommended direction for hiking the Boar Route (PR 2)?
faqs.answer: It is generally recommended to take the trail in a clockwise direction. This approach can help manage the steep inclines and optimize the views along the route.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for children or families?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's moderate difficulty, steep inclines, and some trickier sections, it is best suited for families with older children who have a good level of fitness and hiking experience. It is not recommended for very young children or those new to hiking.

waypoints.name: View of Hornfels Peaks, Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela
